Yazeed Abulaila.bio: The experienced first-choice goalkeeper for Jordan, Abulaila has been a reliable presence between the posts for both Al-Faisaly and the national team. His shot-stopping ability and commanding presence have made him one of the most respected goalkeepers in Jordanian football.
Yazeed Abulaila.playingStyle: Experienced, commanding goalkeeper with strong reflexes, excellent positional sense, and vocal leadership that organizes the defense in front of him
Yazeed Abulaila.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper and defensive leader for Jordan's historic first World Cup; his experience and composure will be tested against the world's best attackers
Yazeed Abulaila.notableFact: Was instrumental in Jordan's run to the 2023 Asian Cup final, producing several key saves that kept the team in knockout matches

Abdallah Nasib.bio: A consistent right-back who has been a regular for Al-Faisaly and the Jordanian national team. Nasib's defensive reliability and willingness to support attacks from the right flank make him an important part of the team's structure.
Abdallah Nasib.playingStyle: Reliable, disciplined right-back with good defensive positioning, consistent tackling, and the ability to join attacks with overlapping runs
Abdallah Nasib.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back who provides defensive stability and controlled attacking support; his familiarity with the national team system makes him a dependable first choice
Abdallah Nasib.notableFact: Part of the Al-Faisaly contingent that forms the backbone of the Jordanian national team, reflecting the club's dominance in domestic football

Ehsan Haddad.bio: A steady left-back playing for Al-Wehdat, one of Jordan's most popular and successful clubs. Haddad provides defensive solidity on the left side and offers balanced support to the team's attacking play.
Ehsan Haddad.playingStyle: Solid, dependable left-back with good defensive awareness, balanced attacking contributions, and tactical discipline within the team's system
Ehsan Haddad.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back who provides defensive structure; his experience at Al-Wehdat, one of Jordan's biggest clubs, has prepared him for high-pressure matches
Ehsan Haddad.notableFact: Plays for Al-Wehdat, the club that historically represents the Palestinian refugee community in Jordan, one of the most passionate fanbases in Asian football

Ahmad Ersan.bio: The veteran defensive leader and one of the most capped players in Jordanian football history. Ersan has been the heart of Jordan's defense for over a decade, providing aerial dominance, leadership, and the kind of experience that cannot be taught.
Ahmad Ersan.playingStyle: Commanding, experienced center-back with dominant aerial presence, strong tackling, and exceptional ability to organize and marshal the defensive line
Ahmad Ersan.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and defensive captain; his vast experience and leadership are irreplaceable for Jordan's first ever World Cup campaign
Ahmad Ersan.notableFact: One of the most capped players in Jordanian history with over 75 appearances, having served as the backbone of the defense through the most successful era in Jordan's football

Anas Bani Yaseen.bio: A strong, experienced center-back who has taken his career to the Gulf leagues with Al-Wakrah in Qatar. Bani Yaseen's aerial prowess, defensive intelligence, and regional experience make him an ideal partner for Ersan in central defense.
Anas Bani Yaseen.playingStyle: Physical, intelligent center-back with strong aerial ability, good reading of the game, and the experience of competing in the competitive Qatar Stars League
keyStrengths: Experience in Gulf football at a high level
Anas Bani Yaseen.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back partner for Ersan, forming a physically dominant and experienced defensive pairing; his Gulf league experience ensures he is accustomed to facing high-quality opposition
Anas Bani Yaseen.notableFact: Scored crucial goals in Jordan's qualifying campaign, demonstrating his aerial threat at set pieces in addition to his defensive capabilities

Salem Al-Ajalin.bio: A combative defensive midfielder who provides the hard-running, ball-winning presence that anchors Jordan's midfield. Al-Ajalin's energy and tactical discipline at Al-Wehdat have translated seamlessly to the national team.
Salem Al-Ajalin.playingStyle: Energetic, combative defensive midfielder who breaks up opposition attacks, covers ground tirelessly, and provides simple, effective distribution to more creative teammates
Salem Al-Ajalin.worldCup2026Role: Starting defensive midfielder who protects the back four; his tireless work rate and ball-winning ability are essential to Jordan's defensive solidity
Salem Al-Ajalin.notableFact: A key member of the Al-Wehdat squad and a regular in the national team, providing the midfield steel that allows Jordan's more creative players to flourish

Mousa Al-Tamari.bio: One of Jordan's most exciting attacking talents, Mousa Al-Tamari is a tricky right winger who has played in European football with Montpellier in Ligue 1. His pace, dribbling ability, and eye for a spectacular goal make him the player opponents fear most.
Mousa Al-Tamari.playingStyle: Explosive, skillful right winger with electric pace, audacious dribbling skills, and the ability to cut inside onto his left foot to shoot or create chances
Mousa Al-Tamari.worldCup2026Role: Starting right winger and primary creative threat; his Ligue 1 experience and individual quality make him the player Jordan look to most for attacking inspiration
Mousa Al-Tamari.notableFact: His performances in French football made him one of the first Jordanian players to establish himself in a top European league, inspiring a new generation of Jordanian footballers

Nizar Al-Rashdan.bio: A reliable central midfielder who has been a consistent performer for Al-Faisaly and the national team. Al-Rashdan's passing ability and tactical awareness provide structure to Jordan's midfield play.
Nizar Al-Rashdan.playingStyle: Composed central midfielder with good passing range, tactical intelligence, and the ability to keep the team organized and moving the ball effectively
Nizar Al-Rashdan.worldCup2026Role: Midfield option who adds composure and passing quality; his ability to control the tempo of play makes him valuable when Jordan need to manage games
Nizar Al-Rashdan.notableFact: Part of the Al-Faisaly contingent that provides the technical core of Jordan's midfield, reflecting the close relationship between club and country

Yazan Al-Naimat.bio: Jordan's most lethal striker, Yazan Al-Naimat has proven himself in European football with BSC Young Boys in Switzerland. His clinical finishing, physical presence, and ability to score at the highest level make him the spearhead of Jordan's attack and their most reliable goal threat.
Yazan Al-Naimat.playingStyle: Clinical, intelligent striker with excellent finishing in the box, good movement to find space, and the physical strength to compete with top-level defenders
keyStrengths: Clinical finishing and composure in front of goal
Yazan Al-Naimat.worldCup2026Role: Starting striker and primary goal threat; his Swiss league experience and proven scoring ability make him the player Jordan rely on most to deliver at the World Cup
Yazan Al-Naimat.notableFact: His move to BSC Young Boys made him one of the first Jordanian players to play in a top European league, and his goal-scoring record in Switzerland has been impressive

Musa Al-Taamari.bio: The captain and heart of Jordanian football, Musa Al-Taamari is a creative midfielder whose vision, passing, and leadership have driven Jordan's rise to World Cup qualification. A veteran of the domestic game with Al-Wehdat, his influence on the team goes far beyond statistics.
Musa Al-Taamari.playingStyle: Intelligent, creative attacking midfielder with exceptional vision, precise passing, excellent set-piece delivery, and the tactical brain to orchestrate the team's attacking play
Musa Al-Taamari.worldCup2026Role: Starting number 10 and creative conductor of Jordan's attack; his ability to find teammates with defense-splitting passes and deliver dangerous set pieces makes him the team's most important playmaker
Musa Al-Taamari.notableFact: Captained Jordan to the 2023 Asian Cup final, the greatest achievement in the nation's football history, cementing his status as the most important player of his generation

Baha Faisal.bio: A prolific domestic striker who has been one of the most consistent goalscorers in the Jordanian Pro League with Al-Wehdat. Faisal's goal-scoring instinct and physical presence provide an important alternative option in attack.
Baha Faisal.playingStyle: Strong, instinctive striker with excellent positioning in the box, physical presence, and a natural goal-scoring touch developed through years of domestic football
Baha Faisal.worldCup2026Role: Key backup striker whose prolific domestic record makes him a reliable option; his aerial presence adds a set-piece threat and his goal-scoring instinct could prove decisive
Baha Faisal.notableFact: One of the top scorers in the Jordanian Pro League in recent seasons, with a remarkable goals-to-games ratio that earns him a crucial squad place

Mohammad Al-Basha.bio: A young goalkeeper developing at Al-Ramtha in the Jordanian Pro League. Al-Basha provides backup goalkeeping depth and represents the future of Jordanian goalkeeping.
Mohammad Al-Basha.playingStyle: Young, athletic goalkeeper with developing skills, good reflexes, and the ambition to challenge for a starting role in future tournaments
Mohammad Al-Basha.worldCup2026Role: Backup goalkeeper gaining invaluable World Cup experience; his inclusion provides depth while investing in the future of Jordanian goalkeeping
Mohammad Al-Basha.notableFact: One of the youngest goalkeepers at the World Cup, gaining once-in-a-lifetime experience that will shape his career for years to come

Tareq Khattab.bio: A solid center-back playing for Al-Salt in the Jordanian Pro League. Khattab provides additional defensive depth with his physical presence and reliable defending.
Tareq Khattab.playingStyle: Physical, reliable center-back with good marking ability, aerial presence, and disciplined positioning within the defensive structure
Tareq Khattab.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option at center-back; his familiarity with the national team setup and reliable defending make him a trusted squad member for emergency situations
Tareq Khattab.notableFact: A dependable domestic league defender who has earned his World Cup squad place through consistent performances for club and country

Hamza Al-Dardour.bio: One of the most prolific scorers in Jordanian football history, Hamza Al-Dardour has been banging in goals for over a decade. His predatory instinct, aerial ability, and unwavering commitment to the national team make him a living legend of Jordanian football.
Hamza Al-Dardour.playingStyle: Veteran, predatory striker with exceptional positioning, aerial prowess, and the killer instinct to convert chances that few others would score
Hamza Al-Dardour.worldCup2026Role: Veteran striker and all-time top scorer whose experience and goal-scoring pedigree make him invaluable; his presence alone lifts the team and terrifies opponents
Hamza Al-Dardour.notableFact: Jordan's all-time leading scorer with over 28 international goals, Al-Dardour's career has defined an era of Jordanian football and his inclusion at the World Cup is a fitting reward

Yousef Al-Rawashdeh.bio: A young defensive midfielder from Al-Hussein who provides additional midfield depth and energy. Al-Rawashdeh's defensive qualities and work rate have earned him a place in the World Cup squad.
Yousef Al-Rawashdeh.playingStyle: Energetic, hardworking defensive midfielder with good tackling ability, willingness to cover ground, and developing positional awareness
Yousef Al-Rawashdeh.worldCup2026Role: Midfield depth option whose energy and defensive qualities make him useful when Jordan need fresh legs or additional midfield protection
Yousef Al-Rawashdeh.notableFact: A product of Al-Hussein's development system, representing the breadth of talent emerging from clubs beyond Jordan's traditional powerhouses

Yazan Abu Arab.bio: A young, technically gifted central midfielder playing for Al-Wehdat. Abu Arab's composure on the ball and passing ability make him an exciting prospect in Jordan's midfield.
Yazan Abu Arab.playingStyle: Technical, composed central midfielder with good ball retention, neat passing, and the ability to keep the team playing through the middle of the park
Yazan Abu Arab.worldCup2026Role: Young midfield talent gaining World Cup experience; his technical quality provides an option when Jordan need to control possession and play through the lines
Yazan Abu Arab.notableFact: Part of the Al-Wehdat academy system that has produced several Jordanian internationals, continuing the club's tradition of developing national team players

Oday Dabbagh.bio: An exciting young left winger who has ventured to Spanish football with Sevilla Atletico. Dabbagh's pace, dribbling ability, and willingness to take on defenders make him one of Jordan's most exciting attacking prospects.
Oday Dabbagh.playingStyle: Quick, direct left winger with good dribbling skills, pace to stretch defenses, and developing end product from wide positions in the Spanish football system
keyStrengths: Directness and willingness to take on defenders
Oday Dabbagh.worldCup2026Role: Exciting wide option whose pace and directness provide an attacking spark from the bench; his experience in Spanish football adds a different dimension to Jordan's attack
Oday Dabbagh.notableFact: One of the first Jordanian players to compete in Spain, joining Sevilla's reserve team and gaining experience in one of the world's most technically demanding football environments

Mohammad Abu Zuraiq.bio: A dependable right-back playing for Al-Faisaly in the Jordanian Pro League. Abu Zuraiq provides additional defensive depth on the right side with his solid defensive qualities.
Mohammad Abu Zuraiq.playingStyle: Solid, defensive-minded right-back with good positional sense, consistent tackling, and reliability in fulfilling his defensive duties
Mohammad Abu Zuraiq.worldCup2026Role: Backup right-back providing reliable squad depth; his experience at Al-Faisaly ensures he is match-ready if called upon
Mohammad Abu Zuraiq.notableFact: Part of the Al-Faisaly defensive contingent that has been central to Jordan's improved results in recent years

Hussain Al-Saify.bio: A young, pacy left winger who has taken his talents to the UAE Pro League with Khor Fakkan. Al-Saify's speed and directness add a dynamic attacking option to Jordan's flanks.
Hussain Al-Saify.playingStyle: Quick, explosive left winger with raw pace, direct running, and the ability to stretch defenses and create space for teammates
Hussain Al-Saify.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking option whose blistering pace can change games from the bench; his ability to run behind defenses provides a valuable alternative approach in attack
Hussain Al-Saify.notableFact: One of the younger players to have ventured to Gulf football, gaining valuable experience in the UAE that has accelerated his development

Ali Olwan.bio: An experienced central midfielder who has been a loyal servant to both Al-Faisaly and the Jordanian national team. Olwan's versatility, work rate, and consistent performances make him a reliable squad member.
Ali Olwan.playingStyle: Versatile, hardworking midfielder with good passing, tactical awareness, and the ability to contribute effectively in various midfield roles
Ali Olwan.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield utility player whose versatility and reliability make him a valuable squad member for tournament football where rotation and tactical flexibility are key
Ali Olwan.notableFact: Has served the national team faithfully for many years, contributing quietly but effectively to Jordan's rise in Asian football

Nawaf Al-Aqeel.bio: A young goalkeeper from Al-Wehdat who represents the future of Jordanian goalkeeping. Al-Aqeel's athletic ability and potential have earned him an early call-up to the World Cup squad.
Nawaf Al-Aqeel.playingStyle: Young, athletic goalkeeper with good reflexes, developing positional sense, and the raw potential to become Jordan's long-term number one
Nawaf Al-Aqeel.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper gaining invaluable World Cup experience at a young age; his inclusion is an investment in Jordan's goalkeeping future
Nawaf Al-Aqeel.notableFact: One of the youngest players in the entire World Cup squad, his selection reflects Jordan's commitment to developing talent for future tournaments

Mohammad Al-Attar.bio: A center-back who has gained experience in Gulf football with Al-Arabi in the Qatar Stars League. Al-Attar's time in Qatar has exposed him to higher-level football and improved his defensive qualities.
Mohammad Al-Attar.playingStyle: Solid, composed center-back with good aerial ability, physical presence, and the composure gained from competing in Qatar's competitive league
Mohammad Al-Attar.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option whose Qatar league experience adds valuable quality; his familiarity with competing against high-level Gulf and international talent prepares him well for World Cup challenges
Mohammad Al-Attar.notableFact: His move to Qatar represents the growing pathway for Jordanian players into Gulf football, providing higher-level experience that benefits the national team

Mahmoud Al-Mardi.bio: A young, exciting right winger from Al-Faisaly who brings pace and energy to Jordan's attacking options. Al-Mardi's directness and youthful exuberance provide an exciting option from the bench.
Mahmoud Al-Mardi.playingStyle: Quick, energetic young winger with good pace, direct running ability, and the fearlessness of youth that allows him to take on experienced defenders
Mahmoud Al-Mardi.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking prospect whose pace and energy make him a useful impact substitute; his World Cup experience at a young age will be invaluable for his development
Mahmoud Al-Mardi.notableFact: One of the youngest members of the squad from the Al-Faisaly academy, representing the next generation of Jordanian football talent stepping onto the world stage
